Meta.title: Hack Club — Where teens make cool stuff.
Meta.description: Hack Club is the world's largest nonprofit movement of teenagers making cool projects.

Common.termsAgree: For all teens aged 13–18. By continuing, you agree to our

Home.stardance: Check out Stardance, the largest free STEM event of the summer
Home.subtext: Hack Club is the world's largest nonprofit movement of teenagers making cool projects.
Home.stickersAria: Get free Hack Club stickers mailed to you — anywhere in the world

Home.donorsTitle: Supported by people who believe in teens.
Home.donorsFree: Hack Club is free for every teenager, forever.
Home.donorsOpen: Hack Club is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit. Our finances are fully open — anyone can
Home.donorsSeeSpend: see exactly how money is spent.
Home.donorsBelieve: If you believe in what we're building,
Home.joiningSubtext: Hack Club isn't just events — here's what you get as part of the community.
Home.joiningSlackBody1: Chat, collaborate, and hang out with tens of thousands of teen makers from around the world. Find people working on the same weird projects as you, get help when you're stuck, or just #lounge.
Home.joiningSlackBody2: We also host AMAs with people like Sal Khan, George Hotz, and Lady Ada — people we really wanted to talk to, so we just invited them.
Home.joiningHcbBody1: Got an idea for a hackathon, club, or event? HCB gives your team the financial tools to accept donations, manage money, and operate under a real 501(c)(3) umbrella — no paperwork nightmare.
Home.joiningHcbBody2: Already used by 700+ teenager-led teams running world-class events
Home.joiningClubsBody1: There are Hack Clubs at high schools all over the world. Each one is a place where you build real projects from day one — no boring lectures, no prerequisites.
Home.joiningClubsBody2: Find one near you, or start your own and we'll help you get it off the ground.
Home.joiningPerksBody: GitHub Education, Brilliant Premium, and a bunch more — all unlocked just by being part of Hack Club. No catch, no cost.
Home.readyBody: Get started with building something real.

Press.metaDescription: Hack Club press resources, media coverage, and official logos for press use.
Press.heroLede: Hack Club is a global nonprofit network of high school makers & student-led coding clubs where young people build the agency, the network, & the technical talent to think big & do big things in the world.
Press.fact1: Global nonprofit network of teen makers.
Press.aboutCopy: Founded in 2014 by 16-year-old Zach Latta, Hack Clubs are now in nearly 1,400 high schools with 100,000 teenagers each year. Hack Club has been profiled on the TODAY Show, in the Wall Street Journal, and in many other publications around the country.
Press.inquiriesCopy: If you are writing a story or have other press inquiries, please contact Christina Asquith.
Press.photosCopy: Download and use these images for coverage of Hack Club programs and events.
Press.logosCopy: Use these official logo files for Hack Club HQ. Stick to SVG, PNG, or PDF. Avoid stretching, outlining, or recoloring.
Press.needMoreCopy: Reach out for photography, event branding, or co-branded work.

Philosophy.metaDescription: Hack Club's philosophy on coding, learning, and building a community of teenage makers.
Philosophy.beliefsTitle: A few beliefs that shape how we build.
Philosophy.joinBody: Start a Hack Club at your school or jump straight into our worldwide community of young makers.

0.title: Coding is a
0.body: Learning to code is uniquely like gaining a superpower: it converts you from a consumer to a creator. Suddenly, computers become a tool for creating.

1.title: Make, from anywhere.
1.body: There's never been a better time for making: anywhere in the world, anyone with a laptop and an internet connection can learn to make an app. Building things has never been so globally democratized.

2.title: Hack, hack, hack.
2.leadText: The goal of Hack Club is to help you become a hacker.
2.body: We want a space at every school where people are making interesting things with code, every week. Most schools don't provide that, so we're creating it in every school to make building things accessible to everyone.

3.title: Start building.
3.body: Most coding classes teach you programming concepts instead of how to write real code. It's like trying to learn carpentry without any wood. So at Hack Club, you learn to code entirely through building things. You start with no experience and build and ship a project every meeting.

4.title: Learn as you build.
4.bodyPrefix: Just as the best carpenters didn't learn in the classroom, neither did the best programmers. Through our
4.bodySuffix: you'll be walked through building projects. Starting out, you won't understand how the code works, but you'll build understanding as you go. You'll get stuck along the way, but we're here to help.

5.title: Be part of a community.
5.body: Hack Club gives you a worldwide community of thousands of other young makers to talk to. We're artists, writers, engineers, tinkerers, campers, filmmakers, volunteers. We make things. We help one another. We have fun. Join us.

Clubs.metaTitle: Hack Club Clubs — Start a Club
Clubs.metaDescription: Start a student-led coding club with Hack Club. Get workshops, mentorship, events, tools, and a global community.
Clubs.heroTitle1: Don't run your coding club alone.
Clubs.heroSubtext: A global nonprofit network of high school coding clubs where teens build technical skills, real projects, and momentum.
Clubs.rundownTitle: Clubs discovering the joy of code.
Clubs.rundownBody: Hack Clubs meet weekly in schools and communities. Leaders kick things off, members build websites, apps, and games at their own pace, then everyone demos what they made.
Clubs.step1Body: Leaders introduce a project direction and get everyone started.
Clubs.step2Body: Members work independently and help each other ship something real.
Clubs.step3Body: Showing your work creates momentum, confidence, and community.
Clubs.rundownFooter: Clubs also run and attend hackathons, join year-long programs, and grow with other student leaders worldwide.
Clubs.welcomeTitle: By the students, for the students.
Clubs.welcomeBody: Learning to code should feel creative and social, not like another class. Every Hack Club is student-led and project-driven.
Clubs.runningTitle: Get your club going and growing.
Clubs.featureLeaderBody: Join thousands of leaders in the Hack Club Slack to share ideas, ask questions, and meet peers in <channel>#leaders</channel>.
Clubs.featureWorkshopsBody: Use ready-to-run meeting content so beginners can build websites, games, bots, and more at <jams>Hack Club Jams</jams>.
Clubs.featureStickersBody: We ship physical materials to help you recruit members and create club culture fast.
Clubs.featureFundingBody: Raise and manage money through Hack Club's nonprofit infrastructure and spend on what your club needs.
Clubs.featureEventsBody: AMAs, hack nights, lock-ins, and weird internet events keep your members inspired all year.
Clubs.featureExistingBody: Already running a CS club? You can join Hack Club and keep your current name while using all our <resources>resources</resources>.
Clubs.startBody: It's online, free, and takes less than an hour to kick off.
Clubs.applyStep1Body: Tell us about your school and who is leading.
Clubs.applyStep2Body: Get approval from your school for the club.
Clubs.applyStep3Body: Schedule your first session and start shipping projects.

Jobs.metaDescription: Join the Hack Club team. View open positions and apply to work with us.
Jobs.heroLede: We are a nonprofit building the best programs and adventures for teenage makers around the world. If you love making things, you will feel right at home.
Jobs.emptyTitle: No open roles at this time. Check back later!
Jobs.emptyBody: Interested in working with us? Email us at team@hackclub.com with how you'd like to contribute.

Arcade.metaDescription: The ultimate summer hackathon for high schoolers. Make projects. Track your hours. Redeem for prizes.
Arcade.heroTagline: The summer is yours for the making
Arcade.heroNote: The Arcade closed September 1st, but you can still join the <slack>Hack Club Slack</slack>!
Arcade.step1Text: Hack on something cool. Examples: making your own PCB, building your own site, creating an app.
Arcade.step2Text: Join the <slack>Hack Club Slack</slack> and use /hack in #arcade to log your hours. You earn a ticket for each hour spent.
Arcade.step3Text: Use your tickets to buy prizes for your next project, like Arduino kits, drawing tablets, and more.
Arcade.stickerLine: Get <stickers>free stickers</stickers> and code with other high schoolers!
Arcade.buildWhateverBody: Any technical project counts. You could build an AR game, pixel art display, drawing robot, and more. Anytime you work on your project, start the hack hour timer. You earn a ticket for every hour.
Arcade.arcadeEndedThanks: Arcade has ended! Thanks for playing.
Arcade.prizesSubtitle: Redeem these with your tickets. For high schoolers or younger only.
Arcade.sneakPeek: This is just a <peek>sneak peek</peek>... there were many more prizes that were unlocked as the summer went on!

projectCards.elijah: Finally shipped my personal AI clone and had a ton of fun seeing what other people did with it.
projectCards.konstantinos: A drone build that can follow the biggest object of a chosen color.

starters.wizardDesc: Build a text-based game with AI.
starters.binDesc: Build an online circuit, get the parts for free.
starters.sprigDesc: Build a JS game, play it on your own console.
starters.onboardDesc: Design a PCB, get a $100 grant.
starters.hackaccinoDesc: Build a 3D website and get a free frappuccino.
starters.blotDesc: Write code. Make art. Get a drawing machine.
starters.ciderDesc: Make a mobile app and get an Apple Developer account.
starters.easelDesc: Write a programming language and receive fudge.

faq.eligibleA: You need to be a high schooler or younger. You just need a GitHub account and a Slack account to participate.
faq.projectsA: Projects need to be open source and have a way for people to experience them. Document progress as you go.
faq.howManyQ: How many projects can I build?
faq.howManyA: You can submit as many projects as you make. We just count the hours.
faq.costA: It is free. Some shipments may have customs charges depending on your country.
faq.helpA: Get help in the #arcade-help channel in the Hack Club Slack.
faq.hoursA: Reviews are done by humans and may take a few days to show up.
faq.teamA: Yes, but your own work needs to be clearly documented.
faq.schoolQ: What about school work or a job?
faq.schoolA: If you are building for school or work, it does not count for Arcade.
faq.scrapA: Code needs a commit. Share links for projects like Sprig, Blot, or hosted 3D models.

Hcb.metaDescription: Start your nonprofit with HCB: a 501(c)(3) legal entity, bank account, automatic taxes and accounting, and best-in-class software.
Hcb.heroBody: Start your nonprofit with our fiscal sponsorship program, HCB: a 501(c)(3) legal entity, bank account, automatic taxes & accounting, and best-in-class software.
Hcb.mobileAlertBody: Manage your HCB organizations on the go. Issue cards, view transactions, and more!
Hcb.pocketBody: The official mobile app lets you manage your organization's finances, issue cards, and more!
Hcb.pocketFeatureSpendingBody: Stay up to date on your organization's balance and transactions.
Hcb.pocketFeatureTapBody: No extra hardware required! Tap any card against your phone.
Hcb.pocketFeatureCardsBody: Add cards to Apple Wallet and Google Wallet directly.
Hcb.pocketFeatureReceiptsBody: Quickly snap a photo or upload a file!
Hcb.feesBody: This fee goes directly to Hack Club's operations staff, including teen interns working under mentors. This allows us to deliver best-in-class software and support, grow sustainably, while also providing paid career training for young people from diverse backgrounds.
Hcb.builtByCollageAlt: Thousands of teenagers attend Hack Club events like this
Hcb.builtByP1: As <home>Hack Club</home> grew, we needed a way to empower our members. We currently have over 60,000 high schoolers involved in Hack Club with over 400 clubs around the world.
Hcb.builtByP2: We started HCB in 2018 to support teen-led clubs and hackathons. After showing it to our educational partners, we knew we had tapped into something much larger. Today, HCB removes financial and legal barriers for thousands doing good in their community.
Hcb.builtByEnvironment: As part of our commitment to the environment, funding for HCB's operations and staff will never come from the <fossil>fossil fuel industry</fossil>.
Hcb.openSourceTitle: Open source infrastructure for fiscally sponsored organizations.
Hcb.openSourceBody: HCB is open source and built in public, like many other Hack Club projects. Join us in building the infrastructure powering fiscally sponsored organizations around the world.
Hcb.gourceAlt: HCB commit history visualised with Gource
Hcb.contactNotice: HCB Visa® Commercial cards are powered by Stripe and issued by Celtic Bank.

HcbFirst.metaTitle: Financial Toolkit for FIRST Teams | HCB
HcbFirst.metaDescription: HCB is the ultimate booster club for FRC, FTC, and FLL teams to receive grants, fundraise, make purchases, and much more.
HcbFirst.heroTitle: The ultimate booster club for <highlight>FRC, FTC, and FLL teams</highlight>.
HcbFirst.heroBody: Built by <first>FIRST</first> alumni for <first>FIRST</first> teams, HCB is a comprehensive financial platform used by hundreds of clubs, teams and hackathons.
HcbFirst.featuresBody:
Organize your team's finances in real time, receive grants, gain nonprofit status, & more.

Use features engineered by FIRST alumni to help you run a successful team.
HcbFirst.dashboardAlt: HCB dashboard shown on a device
HcbFirst.featureNonprofitBody: Become part of Hack Club's legal entity, getting the benefits of our 501(c)(3) tax status.
HcbFirst.featureBalanceBody: Keep everyone on your team and beyond up to date with real-time balance and transaction history.
HcbFirst.featureCardsBody: Issue physical debit cards to all your teammates.
HcbFirst.featureGrantsBody: Easily receive and deposit money from grants and donations into your account. You'll also get a customizable online donation form to share with friends and family.
HcbFirst.featureReimburseBody: Reimburse teammates for expenses with flexible money transfer options including ACH, mailed checks, and more.
HcbFirst.featureSupportBody: With 24-hour response time on weekdays, we'll never leave you hanging.
HcbFirst.testimonialsTitle: <first>FIRST</first> teams all over the country run on HCB.
HcbFirst.testimonialsBody: Everywhere from San Jose to Boston to New York, HCB powers teams of all sizes.
HcbFirst.applyBody: Open to Hack Clubs, hackathons, and charitable organizations in the US and Canada.
HcbFirst.runHq: We run Hack Club HQ on HCB!

HcbMobile.metaTitle: HCB Mobile is here! — Hack Club
HcbMobile.metaDescription: Manage your HCB organizations on the go. Issue cards, view transactions, and more!
HcbMobile.heroSubtitle: Manage your HCB organizations on the go. Issue cards, view transactions, and more!
HcbMobile.p1: I'm Mohamad, a 17-year-old from the SF Bay Area, and I just shipped the official mobile app for HCB.
HcbMobile.p2: If you haven't heard of it, HCB is the financial backbone for over 6,500 teenager-led nonprofits, clubs, and hackathons. We provide 501(c)(3) nonprofit status, access to a bank account, a donation collection platform, and debit cards for thousands of young people trying to do good in their communities.
HcbMobile.p3: HCB is currently processing an average of $6 million per month (over $80M in its lifetime)1. For the last year, I've led the project to build the first-ever mobile app for this entire community.
HcbMobile.p4: The entire project is open source on GitHub (we'd love a ⭐️!).
HcbMobile.whyBody: These teenagers are running robotics teams, hackathons, and nonprofit projects that improve their community. They need a way to manage their organization's finances from their pocket. With HCB Mobile, they'll be able to:
HcbMobile.whyLi1: Track their organization's balance and transactions on the go.
HcbMobile.whyLi2: Accept in-person tap-to-pay donations, perfect for an in-person fundraiser or event! No extra hardware required. Just tap any credit/debit card against your phone.
HcbMobile.whyLi3: Issue new debit cards, add them to Apple / Google Wallet, and freeze or cancel them directly from their phone.
HcbMobile.whyLi4: Upload receipts directly from their device or match existing receipts in Receipt Bin to transactions with a tap.
HcbMobile.techP1: When I started working on this app, I wanted to build in native code like SwiftUI for iOS and Kotlin/Jetpack Compose for Android. However, I realized that it would be a pain for me, a full-time student with classes, to handle two codebases. I'd have to duplicate every feature I created for one OS to the other and deal with all the integration issues along the way. Then, I discovered Expo (a React Native framework) which allowed me to write one app that worked on multiple devices. Working with Expo, I learned about creating my own Expo Modules (to bridge native code features to Typescript) and optimization methods like memoization and component recycling.
HcbMobile.techP2: The non-code side of this app was no joke, either. I had to work with the Apple and Google app review teams to obtain restricted entitlements for features like mobile tap-to-pay terminal provisioning (made possible by Stripe) and push provisioning (which allows users to add cards to their payment wallet directly from HCB Mobile). It took several months and many back-and-forth email chains to finally get the entitlements we needed.
HcbMobile.techP3: After over 250 hours of development work, I can say that I'm incredibly proud of HCB Mobile because it's built by teenagers to make it easier for teenagers like me to run nonprofit organizations and projects with HCB. Beyond teenagers, HCB also supports hundreds of adult-run organizations such as mutual aid groups, open source projects, and community spaces.
HcbMobile.appStoreAria: Download HCB on the App Store
HcbMobile.footnote: This amount is excluding HQ (our own) <finances>finances</finances>.
HcbMobile.ctaBody: We're accepting applications! No startup fees, no minimum balance, and no long wait time.

HcbOpenSource.metaTitle: HCB is now open source! | Hack Club
HcbOpenSource.metaDescription: Our fiscal sponsorship platform's codebase is now publicly available under the AGPL license and we're continuing to encourage transparency amongst nonprofits.
HcbOpenSource.heroSubtitle: Our fiscal sponsorship platform's <codebase>codebase</codebase> is now publicly available under the AGPL license and we're continuing to encourage transparency amongst nonprofits.
HcbOpenSource.p1: Hack Club launched HCB in 2018 to enable hackathons to raise and spend money through <fs>fiscal sponsorship</fs>. Since then, we've expanded to all nonprofit projects; our 12,000 users have transacted $50 million.
HcbOpenSource.uiAlt: HCB's user interface showing Hack Club HQ's transactions
HcbOpenSource.p2: Local student-run hackathons, robotics teams, and community groups use HCB as a nonprofit neobank to collect donations, send payments, issue debit cards, and gain 501(c)(3) status.
HcbOpenSource.p3: When we started HCB, it was developed in private for security reasons. That said, one of Hack Club's core principles has always been transparency — we <opensource>open source</opensource> our <finances>finances</finances>, <events>document how we run events</events>, and have 500+ public repositories on <github>GitHub</github>.
HcbOpenSource.p4: github.com/hackclub/hcb is now public — check it out and star it.
HcbOpenSource.p5: Paired with our technical documentation, it's a great resource for anyone interested in building financial software or applications with Ruby on Rails. Our engineering work is also entirely public; the world can learn from our successes and mistakes.
HcbOpenSource.p6: Since 2018, over fifty people have made 10k+ commits to HCB (thank you!); we can't wait for more contributors to join us:
HcbOpenSource.videoFallback: Your browser does not support the video tag.
HcbOpenSource.ps: PS: if you're looking to start a nonprofit, we're accepting applications! Head over to <nonprofit>nonprofit.new</nonprofit> and we'll be in touch.
HcbOpenSource.photoAlt: Hack Club team launching the announcement live from San Francisco
HcbOpenSource.photoCaption: We're launching this live from SF!
HcbOpenSource.welcomeBody: Hack Club is a global community of high school makers & student-led coding clubs. We've got a 24/7 Slack chatroom of 104k+ teenagers learning to code & building amazing projects, & you'll fit right in.

HcbFeatures.headline: Powerful financial tools built by our nonprofit, for yours.
HcbFeatures.subtext: Since day one, we've built beautiful, self-serve software to empower you to raise and spend money without administrative hassle. We're also open source!
HcbFeatures.openSourceGithub: See our open source on GitHub
HcbFeatures.laptopAlt: HCB dashboard shown in a laptop

Team.metaDescription: Meet the Hack Club team — the people behind the world's largest nonprofit network of teen coders.
Team.heroLede: Hack Club runs on a mix of staff, gap years, and teen contributors building programs, infrastructure, community rituals, and helping with the day-to-day magic behind the scenes.
Team.zachBio: Zach founded Hack Club after dropping out of high school to build software used by millions. He's been awarded the Thiel Fellowship and Forbes 30 Under 30.
Team.christinaBio: Christina cofounded Hack Club after 20 years as a journalist/war correspondent, reporting from Iraq, Afghanistan and Africa, and founding The Fuller Project, an investigative journalism nonprofit focused on women. She holds an MA in philosophy from London School of Economics and has published 2 nonfiction books.
Team.coreTeamsCopy: HQ helps run the main Hack Club experience, while HCB keeps thousands of fiscally sponsored non-profits running. Both are made up of staff, gap years, and teen contributors.
Team.hqDescription: The generalist operating team behind the main Hack Club experience.
Team.hcbDescription: The team keeping nonprofits, reimbursements, and back-office operations moving.
Team.communityTitle: The teen-facing crews shaping daily culture.
Team.communityCopy: These teams are the visible edge of Hack Club: welcoming people in, moderating the space, hosting events, and telling the story of what everyone is making together.
Team.podModerationDescription: Keeping spaces safe, welcoming, and healthy as the community grows.
Team.podWelcomersDescription: The first hello for new Hack Clubbers showing up in the Slack.
Team.podVirtualEventsDescription: Running online moments, experiments, and community-wide activities.
Team.podNewspaperDescription: Publishing stories about the community and showcasing makers.

Philanthropy.metaDescription: Support Hack Club's mission to build a generation of teen makers. Learn how to donate and get involved.
Philanthropy.heroLede: Led by young engineers, Hack Club already reaches tens of thousands of teenagers. With your support, Hack Club can grow to hundreds of thousands more.
Philanthropy.taxCaption: Your contribution is tax-deductible. Hack Club is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit (EIN: 81-2908499).
Philanthropy.visionTitle: We back teens with big ideas.
Philanthropy.visionIntro: In the next ten years, Hack Club will discover, foster, and inspire thousands more teenagers to use technical skills to solve problems.
Philanthropy.visionP1: Hack Club is always free for teenagers. With your support, Hack Club can bring free computer science education, a hacker mindset, and an equal shot at success to every teenager, regardless of where they are from.
Philanthropy.visionP2: Over time, Hack Clubbers will reshape societies as entrepreneurs, environmentalists, political leaders, activists, and policy makers. We help shape the values of these future leaders.
Philanthropy.visionConclusion: We need your support to make this vision a reality.
Philanthropy.visionQuote: “With major support, I am confident Hack Club will change the world.”
Philanthropy.testimonialJason: I've met some of the best people
Philanthropy.testimonialSam: In Hack Club I've found a home
Philanthropy.testimonialAbby: Helped build me a strong coding foundation
Philanthropy.testimonialAdriano: Totally different from coding classes at school
Philanthropy.giftContact: Christina Asquith, Co-founder, COO, and Board Member

Philanthropy.acceptOther: We also accept crypto, stocks, and other forms of support.
Philanthropy.communityTitle: Join our community of generous donors.
Philanthropy.donorsNote: * number in parentheses indicates gifts since 2018.
Philanthropy.supportersTitle: A few others who support Hack Club
Philanthropy.serviceTitle: These fabulous companies donate their products to us.
Philanthropy.impactObreyIntro: Obrey started a Hack Club in his senior year at Kabulonga Boys' Secondary School. Soon, a dozen students were coding every week.
Philanthropy.impactObreyQuote: Thanks to our donor-funded laptop program, Hack Club sent him a MacBook Air. In his club, Obrey and his best friend Edward built robots that won Canada's Humanitarian Activist Award.
Philanthropy.impactMaggieIntro: Maggie joined Hack Club in 2021 and has since shipped projects from widgets to Raycast extensions.
Philanthropy.impactMaggieQuote: "Hack Club inspired me to step outside my comfort zone and take on challenges I never previously would have - starting a CS Club at my school, co-hosting AMAs, and organizing Leland Hacks."
Philanthropy.belleQuote: “Hack Club helped me fall in love with creating and made me feel like I belong.”
Philanthropy.newsTitle: As the largest network of technical teenagers, we are featured in global press.
Philanthropy.boardZachRole: Founder and Executive Director, Hack Club
Philanthropy.boardAdvisor: Board advisor: John Abele (Founder, Boston Scientific)
Philanthropy.boardQuote1: “Hack Club is the organization I wish I had when I was a teenager. In 2017, I joined as a founding board member, and I have seen firsthand the leadership team act with integrity and transparency since Day 1.”
Philanthropy.boardQuote2: “Founder Zach Latta and COO Christina Asquith are efficient, responsible and disciplined stewards of every dollar, and I have proudly grown my donations over the years.”
Philanthropy.boardQuoteAttr: — Tom Preston-Werner, Co-founder and former CEO, GitHub
Philanthropy.fundsTitle: Direct support for teenagers, clubs, and projects.
Philanthropy.funds1: Increase support to serve thousands more teenagers, with a strong focus on teens who face additional barriers.
Philanthropy.funds2: Create hundreds more Hack Clubs in high schools and communities across the world.
Philanthropy.funds3: Inspire a problem-solving mindset where teenagers build what they want to see.
Philanthropy.funds4: Host dozens of in-person events, including flagship summer adventures.
Philanthropy.funds5: Extend mini-grants of hardware and internet access to hundreds of teenagers.
Philanthropy.funds6: Grow the team, mostly engineers, to meet demand from teens worldwide.
Philanthropy.transparencyBody: Starting in 2021, Hack Club has engaged with an external auditing firm and has audited financials through the current fiscal year.
Philanthropy.form990Pending: 2025 form will be shared when ready.
Philanthropy.ctaTitle: Hack Club invites leading thinkers, builders, and disrupters to join us.
Philanthropy.ctaBody: We envision thousands of diverse Hack Club leaders in towns and cities across America and the world, connected online and self-organizing events and hackathons.
Philanthropy.sigZach: Zach Latta, Founder and Executive Director
